title: ImprovementCandidate
description: A reviewable artifact produced by an adjacent skill (/friction, /behave, /skeptic, /dne, /evolve, /genius, /go, manual) proposing a change to /go or another skill. A passing validate_improvement_candidate.py run means the file is well-formed. It does NOT approve, schedule, or auto-promote the candidate. Review is a separate human step.

candidate_id.description: Format: IC-<KEY>-<LOCAL>. KEY is the source skill (BEH/DNE/EVL/FRI/GEN/GO/MAN/SKP). LOCAL is a slug (1-64 chars from [A-Za-z0-9._:-]).

source_session_or_run_id.description: Optional pointer to the session/run that surfaced the candidate.

evidence.description: Citations: file:line, command output, transcript excerpt, or 'not found'. REQUIRED for verified/inferred tiers.

evidence_tier.description: Strength of the strongest citation in `evidence`. `confidence` must be calibrated against this tier.

frequency.description: How often the problem was observed: 'once', 'N times in window', 'always', etc. Free-text; structured counts are the producer's responsibility.

affected_layer.description: Layer the problem lives on. May differ from target_layer (e.g., problem on `hook`, change on `prompt_only`).

target_skill_or_system.description: Name of the skill/system the proposal targets (e.g. 'go', 'dne', 'orchestrator').

proposed_change.description: Concrete description of the proposed change. NOT implementation; this is a reviewable artifact.

target_layer.description: Layer the proposal, if implemented, would modify. Drives the mechanism_trace and promotion_requirements requirements.

mechanism_trace.description: Structured producer → artifact/state → consumer → authority → freshness → failure-direction → real-boundary-test trace. Required (non-null with null_reason OR full trace object) when target_layer ∈ {runtime_gate, hook, orchestrator, validation_script}. May be JSON null or the null-variant object for pure ideas/observations on lower-risk layers.

confidence.description: Calibrated against evidence_tier: high requires execution_artifact/official_doc_or_spec/source_inspection/test_result + reproduction.

risk.description: Risk of acting on the candidate without further review.

failure_mode_prevented.description: What failure this candidate is expected to prevent or reduce.

falsification_condition.description: The observation/measurement that would show this candidate is wrong or ineffective.

reviewer_acceptance.description: True iff a reviewer has signed off. False for proposed/needs_evidence candidates.

evidence_basis.description: What evidence the reviewer should weigh.

items.description: Checklist the reviewer must complete before promotion. Required keys depend on target_layer (see validator). When review_status='implemented', every item must have satisfied=true and evidence.

evidence.description: Citation when satisfied=true. Required for review_status='implemented'.

recommended_destination.description: Layer the candidate's author recommends acting on first.

review_status.description: Where this candidate sits in the review lifecycle. 'proposed' does NOT imply implementation; promotion requires a separate, explicit task.

authority_or_verdict.description: Who decides the verdict (e.g. 'PreToolUse gate', 'deterministic continuation gate').

failure_direction.description: Where the failure goes on missing/stale (silent allow / hard block / advisory-only).

real_boundary_test.description: How the claim is verified at the real boundary, not a unit test (e.g. 'spawned Agent() with tools omitted from schema').
